task_completion.visual_evidence: The title 'NOC Wall Display' is visible at the top of the Graphite dashboard interface.

task_completion.description: Graph 'RDS CPU Risk' contains the styled CPU utilization metric
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'RDS CPU Risk' exists. Its target list or legend shows 'servers.rds_database.cpu.utilization' with functions applying a line width of 3 and color 'blue' (e.g., color(lineWidth(..., 3), 'blue')). The graph visually displays a thick blue line.

task_completion.description: Graph 'RDS CPU Risk' contains a red critical threshold line at 85
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'RDS CPU Risk' graph has a second target using threshold(85) or constantLine(85), styled with color 'red' and aliased as 'Critical'. A horizontal red line is visually present at the y-axis value of 85.

task_completion.description: Graph 'Traffic Imbalance' contains the styled absolute difference of traffic sensors
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Traffic Imbalance' exists. Its target list shows absolute(diffSeries(servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_1, servers.web_traffic.speed_sensor_2)) with functions applying a line width of 2 and color 'orange'. The graph visually displays an orange line representing this computed delta.

task_completion.description: Graph 'Traffic Imbalance' contains a yellow tolerance threshold line at 500
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Traffic Imbalance' graph has a second target using threshold(500) or constantLine(500), styled with color 'yellow' and aliased as 'Tolerance'. A horizontal yellow line is visually present at the y-axis value of 500.

task_completion.description: The dashboard is saved with both graphs present
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Graphite interface shows the dashboard in a saved state with both the 'RDS CPU Risk' and 'Traffic Imbalance' graphs visible simultaneously on the screen.

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Graphite web UI being interacted with, rather than a terminal, script, or text editor modifying dashboard JSON files directly.

integrity.description: The graphs are genuinely rendered by the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs show actual data lines and axes corresponding to the metrics and thresholds, not just empty boxes or text placeholders.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy-paste the task description into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors open containing the raw prompt or task description used to fake completion.
